title: Electrical and Electronics Repairers, Powerhouse, Substation, and Relay
description: Inspect, test, repair, or maintain electrical equipment in generating stations, substations, and in-service relays.

tasks.text: Inspect and test equipment and circuits to identify malfunctions or defects, using wiring diagrams and testing devices such as ohmmeters, voltmeters, or ammeters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Prepare and maintain records detailing tests, repairs, and maintenance.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Consult manuals, schematics, wiring diagrams, and engineering personnel to troubleshoot and solve equipment problems and to determine optimum equipment functioning.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Analyze test data to diagnose malfunctions, to determine performance characteristics of systems, or to evaluate effects of system modifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Open and close switches to isolate defective relays, performing adjustments or repairs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Notify facility personnel of equipment shutdowns.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair, replace, and clean equipment and components such as circuit breakers, brushes, and commutators.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Run signal quality and connectivity tests for individual cables, and record results.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Maintain inventories of spare parts for all equipment, requisitioning parts as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Construct, test, maintain, and repair substation relay and control systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test insulators and bushings of equipment by inducing voltage across insulation, testing current, and calculating insulation loss.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Schedule and supervise the construction and testing of special devices and the implementation of unique monitoring or control systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Schedule and supervise splicing or termination of cables in color-code order.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Test oil in circuit breakers and transformers for dielectric strength, refilling oil periodically.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Disconnect voltage regulators, bolts, and screws, and connect replacement regulators to high-voltage lines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
